text: Morning 7am crew, Yesterday was a tough one - price action during the London session was frustrating. My view remains that we’ve got one or more price-intensive sellers pushing hard to force liquidity. Maybe it’s someone cutting a loss, maybe it’s early IPO buyers cashing out, who knows. There’s no point speculating; we can’t know for certain. What I do know for certain is this: Smarter Web is in the strongest position it’s ever been. Today marks the last working day of August, and it’s worth pausing to recognise just how much the team and community have achieved this month: •Successful capital raise •Convert launch •ATM now working •Jesse and other hires confirmed •Fresh merch live •More Bitcoin stacked •Interviews from AW and JM •Respected names buying the dip And a community that’s growing, in both numbers and collective energy. Yes, volatility is here, and we should be prepared that it could stick around a little longer but once sentiment shifts back, we are positioned to flourish. I’m keeping a close eye on Metaplanet as they are making some big moves which will could bring a lot of attention back to BTCTCs (see posts from @ZynxBTC ). So keep the faith. Stay focused on the fundamentals. Keep engaging in the group by asking questions, sharing views etc. Remember that this asset and sector are cyclical - by nature, they must (and will) rise again and when they do, we’ll all be here for it.

text: Understanding the $SWC price action: flow, sentiment and fundamentals Getting a few questions around what’s happening with $SWC , when the current sell-off might end, etc. A few thoughts: 1. Not a new development: This sell-off isn’t something that started today; it’s part of the ongoing compression we’ve seen across BTCTCs over the past two months. We had some stabilisation around the 2x mNAV level (currently 1.9x), which was encouraging. I still think this level will act as the “pin” in the short term, with trading likely to range around that level (1.8x–2.2x) until sentiment fully improves. 2. Flow dynamics: It feels like one (or more) price-insensitive sellers continue to hit bids to create liquidity. My view is that it's unlikely an institution, as they usually follow stricter guidelines and more likely an early investor or IPO buyer exiting for cash. Ultimately, speculating on why someone sells is pointless, it just happens. 3. $SWC specific news flow remains positive: Over the last two months, and especially this week, $SWC has delivered only good news: the ATM raise, today’s buy announcement, an auditor change, AW/JM in HK, and plenty of community engagement which has continued to expand. This supports points 1 & 2 – the current weakness reflects the broader trend, not any negative company specific development. When will it stop? Nobody can call the exact bottom. My view is that we’re close to an inflection point, but the timing depends on: • Bitcoin price • BTCTC sector sentiment • Flows/volume • Broader market sentiment • $SWC -specific news flow What can you do? Stop checking the price every five minutes (easier said than done). Yes, we could go lower in the short term, but what matters are the fundamentals: we’ve stacked 2,440 Bitcoin in just over four months. If you believe Bitcoin keeps rising, that value will be reflected in the share price, even if we assume a 1x mNAV. For perspective, if Bitcoin reaches $1m by 2030 (a target shared by Coinbase, Ark, Fidelity, among others), $SWC would be worth ~650p (above this years ATH), assuming no growth beyond today’s stack and current share count. It takes patience, which is always the hardest part. Everyone at Smarter Web and across the community is working tirelessly to raise funding and build awareness. It’s important to trust the process, even when it feels challenging. The volatility we’re experiencing now is the same force that ultimately drives the upside. I wrote yesterday that Smarter Web would inevitably have to go through it's first full cycle of mNAV expansion and compression - it’s part of the journey for any BTCTC. Once we’ve navigated this cycle, it will strengthen our credibility and make future investor conversations far easier, as we’ll have proven our ability to manage a full market cycle successfully.

text: Positive research note from Tennyson Securities - maintain BUY, stock is attractively valued. The UK’s flagship Bitcoin Treasury Company, The Smarter Web Company announced a £3.7m Bitcoin purchase just days after confirming a £3.7 million equity raise from its ATM facility. This increases the company’s Bitcoin stack from 2,395 to 2,440, making it the 25th biggest public company globally in terms of Bitcoin on the balance sheet. The purchase takes the company’s 30-day Bitcoin yield to 28% (or 0.9% per day). Since its April IPO (127 days ago), SWC has raised £202m of external capital, 99% of which has been deployed into Bitcoin purchases. The company’s Bitcoin treasury is valued at £204m, reflecting a gain of over £3.3 million since going public with an IPO valuation of just £3.7 million. Analysts cite $SWC 's strong execution of its Bitcoin Treasury strategy as the reason it is viewed as “the fastest horse.” Despite mNAV compression affecting sentiment across BTC treasury companies in August, today’s news highlights the ATM facility as a key competitive differentiator. $SWC remains the only UK-listed Bitcoin treasury company with such a facility, enabling equity raises at market to fund accretive Bitcoin purchases. This capability allows $SWC to sustain its Bitcoin yield even under tougher market conditions. With an mNAV of 1.9x and P/BYD of 0.2x, the stock remains attractively valued. The recommendation is maintained at BUY.

text: To establish itself as one of the “Big Three” alongside Strategy and Metaplanet, Smarter Web needed to undergo this phase of mNAV expansion and compression - it’s a necessary rite of passage to prove it could not only survive, but ultimately thrive as a successful BTCTC. Strategy and Metaplanet have been through this multiple times. Consider it a form of initiation, a kind of Spartan agoge, that every BTCTC must endure. You’re not just witnessing it, you’re part of it. Coming out the other side of this cycle will significantly enhance Smarter Web’s credibility with both existing and prospective institutional investors. It demonstrates that the flywheel works not only in bullish periods but throughout the full mNAV cycle. And for the community, this will bring deeper confidence - if we’ve done it once, we can do it again. Next time, it’ll be easier, both operationally and emotionally and we will have learned so much collectively. In many ways, this was always the destined path for Smarter Web and for @asjwebley as CEO. The fortunate part is that we reached this stage with the flywheel already turning at a sustainable scale, with a great team including @Croesus_BTC and others to join and with a strong community behind us. We are very close to or at the end of this volatile period and will emerge even stronger to smash Q4 together!

text: Some thoughts on this week and a letter of appreciation to @asjwebley

engagement.note: These figures were correct as of May 14, 2026, 9:00 AM London time

text: Andrew, thank you for the thoughtful update. It’s easy for CEOs to communicate when everything is going well but it takes real leadership to step forward when sentiment is shaky. This update speaks volumes about your character and commitment. These moments were always going to come (and will come again) for both Smarter Web and you as CEO - I think you’ve handled this period with grace, clarity, and a high level of visibility. The amount of work being done, much of it behind the scenes, has not gone unnoticed. Thank you especially for doing so many live talks this week. That kind of visibility and engagement makes a real difference. I'm also really glad you were able to listen to the Spaces we hosted. With the recent price action and broader market pressure, it felt important to provide some context and reassurance. Both @ZynxBTC and I received positive feedback, and we’re planning to do more of these going forward. Hopefully, the takeaway was clear: what’s happening with SWC isn't isolated. It’s part of a broader, sector-wide mNAV compression and the key point is that, thanks to excellent execution over the past few months, Smarter Web is in a strong position to ride out this turbulence. We have a profitable operating business, we can continue raising capital (most likely via the ATM, which remains accretive at 2x mNAV), and we’re still able to accumulate Bitcoin, even if at a slower pace for now. One of the reasons often cited for the recent compression is market saturation with too many new entrants. Not all of them will make it through this phase. While it’s tough to see now with current price action, I do believe this correction will ultimately prove healthy for the sector and a net positive for Smarter Web as weaker, opportunistic startups fall away. I keep coming back to the excellent progress map shared by @DrBitcoinM50862 . It’s remarkable to see how much has been accomplished over the summer, traditionally a quiet period. That gives me strong conviction that, once sentiment turns, Smarter Web will be in a prime position to outperform. More broadly, I expect the remaining BTCTCs to continue trading in a recurring cycle of mNAV compression and expansion, possibly several times a year. Metaplanet is a great example of this, and their chart shows how this behavior plays out (see below). Because this is our first cycle, it naturally feels uncomfortable. But once we accept that this volatility is a feature, and the very thing that enables outsized returns during expansions, it becomes easier to navigate emotionally, especially as future compressions will come from a higher base (both in share price and Bitcoin holdings). I also want to say how impressed I’ve been with the community this week. Despite the price action, I’ve never been prouder to be a shareholder. While other communities are experiencing some tensions, SWC’s engagement has been constructive, thoughtful, and overwhelmingly supportive. We’ve built one of the most authentic and organic groups in the space, and how we've responded during this period is exactly the kind of behaviour one should expect from a company with real substance and quality. I understand the challenge you’re facing around capital raising. Jesse’s full-time appointment was a major win and I’m glad you have him as a sounding board. Personally, I still view raising capital here as accretive, especially when you look at the average mNAV levels across prior raises. Even if we raise at 2x now, the impact on the overall average is minimal. That said, I fully trust your judgement on what’s best for Smarter Web in the long run. The execution so far has been exceptional. I really do believe we’re nearing an inflection point. Once we move past August and into September, when volume and risk appetite typically return, I expect the narrative and pricing to shift meaningfully. You’ve done a tremendous job this week, and I just want to say that it’s noticed and deeply appreciated. Let’s keep reminding ourselves of just how far Smarter Web has come since IPO, and take confidence in the fact that the strong fundamentals will eventually reflect in the price. Onwards and upwards, together.

text: Call notes between @asjwebley and @RoxomTV yesterday. TLDR: Andrew is more confident than ever in Smarter Web’s trajectory, with strong balance sheet growth, rising investor interest, and a clear strategy to scale through disciplined capital raises and quality hires. * All interpretations are my own Investor Engagement & Market Sentiment • Andrew has a full pipeline of investor meetings over the next two months. • As $SWC grows, capital raising announcements will not follow a linear pattern raise sizes and sources will vary depending on stage and opportunity. • UK investor sentiment has shifted significantly over the past 3.5 months. Buy-side firms now feel they can start understanding the business, and meetings feel more constructive. • Peel Hunt yesterday released a research report on BTCTCs (I haven't seen it yet), further validating market interest. • $SWC 's ability to consistently raise capital via existing and new methods continues to build credibility with new and potential investors. Market & Performance • BTCTCs experience cycles similar to Bitcoin’s but on a more compressed and intense timeline. • Factors impacting the current environment include the August summer lull and some saturation from new entrants. • Despite a pullback since the June peak (driven by BTCTC euphoria), Smarter Web remains +70x from IPO. • Balance sheet has grown to £200m, with continued execution on capital raising through both existing channels and new structures (e.g., Smarter Convert). • These actions reinforce credibility with investors and support the “flywheel effect” of increasing Bitcoin per share. • Long-term ambition: Smarter Web to be one of the largest BTCTCs, targeting 1% of total Bitcoin supply. • Andrew does not provide a formal year-end BTC price target but expects it to be higher; current internal benchmark is 4,200. Bitcoin Holdings Strategy • Andrew does not believe in lending out Smarter Web’s Bitcoin holdings, given the level of risk involved today. • In the future, there may be opportunities to design safer solutions or products that leverage their Bitcoin treasury but this is years into the future. Team & Hiring • Strong emphasis on building capacity with quality hires, while keeping the team lean and focused. Andrew believes this mix will provide the right capacity and balance to continue scaling. Five key hires in progress (no more hiring expected in 2025): • Marketing Manager – starting September 1. • Executive Assistant Role – beginning September 8, allowing Andrew to focus more on strategic work. • Operating Business – new hire joining in coming weeks; shout-out to Alex for leadership here. • Undisclosed Role – confirmed to start before year-end. • Strategic Hire – announcement soon to signal Smarter Web’s future direction. Operating Business Momentum • Higher profile has led to a noticeable increase in inbound inquiries (particularly evident in August). • A key strength is the diversified client base: many small clients rather than a reliance on a few large ones. • Andrew is excited to see how this momentum develops over the next 12 months. Leadership Perspective • Andrew expressed unprecedented confidence in Smarter Web’s trajectory. • Reinforced commitment to treating all investors equally. • He is particularly excited about the coming weeks and months.

text: Hi Andrew, As always, I wanted to take a moment to say how impressed I’ve been with everything you and the team have achieved over the past month, especially during one of the quietest periods of the year. While we all see the results, I’m acutely aware of the immense effort behind the scenes (much of which never comes to light), along with the early mornings and late finishes. It’s great having Jesse on board, he adds a different perspective, and it’s obvious how much he’s enjoying being part of the Smarter Web mission. I can see how valuable it is to have someone to bounce ideas off. I spend a lot of time looking at other communities, and I can confidently say ours is one of the most organic and engaged. Your presence and comments go a long way in making people feel seen, and the Saturday write-ups though I know they must take up a lot of your time are a true differentiator that people really value. I also admire the way you back yourself and your vision - your humble yet confident tone is inspiring, and I love the ambitious growth targets you’ve set for Smarter Web. I think you have to "go big or go home" and this is the time to be courageous - everything you've done so far should give you the confidence to be bold with your ambition and everyone will back you. It was great to hear on the interview that you’ve hired an EA - that should give you more time to focus on the things that matter most and I look forward to seeing the impact the new marketing manager makes. Either way, I just wanted to say well done again. I’m a very happy shareholder and deeply appreciate all the hard work and dedication you’re putting in to change live, not just for our community, but for the UK as a whole.

text: Notes from another great call between @asjwebley and @ourgoodlifeuk TLDR: $SWC continues to build momentum. Lots of encouraging conversations in different areas and lots of progress made over August extremely encouraging. * Below notes are my own interpretation. The Smarter Convert (TSC) • Andrew is very pleased with TSC’s structure. Tobam were supportive. • Believes TSC is a highly attractive product for SWC. • Current capacity capped at $60m (max 30% of treasury size) with strong demand from a diverse investor base. • Next issuance expected on slightly better terms for SWC. • Separately exploring a potentially first-of-its-kind product. • Will speak to advisers about sharing a “mini deck” to further explain TSC. • TSC is included in all investor conversations but not marketed separately. • For qualified investors, minimum participation ~10–15 BTC; smaller tickets can be pooled. ATM Facility • Shard executing strongly, consistently outperforming VWAP. • 20% of daily volume appears optimal. • Mechanics expected to remain broadly the same. • No plans to pause the ATM to “let the stock breathe” (I agree with this). Placing vs Subscription Difference • Placing: via broker (often with commission). • Subscription: direct with company (minimal/no commission). • Subscription generally preferable as it avoids commission costs. Market & Investor Sentiment • Sees ETN news as positive - boosts Bitcoin’s legitimacy and shows UK as forward-thinking. • Market liquidity can feel thin when buying Bitcoin as noticed by Andrew. • Rising BTC price is optimal for SWC => Increases treasury value, makes capital raising easier and a rising share price means less dilution from capital markets activity. Capital Raising & Investor Engagement • ABBs very much remain part of the plan; August is simply seasonally quieter for investors. • Encouraged by volume of meetings with potential investors. •Recent conversations with an American bank; more meetings with UK institutions lately. • Many institutions prefer to back only one BTC treasury company (BTCTC). • US investors remain especially important given capital depth; surprising comfort investing via Aquis when they understand SWC’s future plans. Listing Plans • No concrete plans for Nasdaq listing. • Possible dual listing in the long term, but not for 2025; careful narrative management would be essential. Acquisition Strategy and Operating Business • Scope has broadened since IPO but same core criteria: • Looking for businesses with Low risk, higher than 30% annual growth, Payback in ≤3 years, Medium/long-term cash generation potential. • Will only do a deal that works for the company - may take time to come through but they are always looking and no announcements here shouldn't be taken as a sign that nothing is happening. • Strong growth potential in operating business; robust ramp-up plan in place. BTC Strategy • Doesn't want to give a fixed year-end BTC target. Noted that Strategy don't have a target except to “buy forever.” •Think a better target would be dynamic (e.g., doubling from last achieved figure: 2,100 → 4,200 → 8,400 BTC). • Avoids being pinned to a time-specific goal. Other Updates • Financial statements to remain bi-annual (UK standard). • Possible AGM event in Feb/March 2026. • Would like more mainstream media coverage - growth here will remain organic; no PR firm engaged to date but expect marketing manager hire to help aid this. • Executive assistant joining end-September (expected to free up ~2 hours/day). • Current team and hiring plans seen as sufficient to cover all key areas. • Medium-to-long term outlook: bullish; volatility is to be embraced.

text: Just a quick reminder: financial markets are not always efficient. Good news isn’t always priced in right away. Take Metaplanet, for example - yesterday they reported their strongest earnings quarter ever, accompanied by a compelling investor presentation outlining their forward strategy. Yet the stock closed down 5%. It’s a relevant case study because there will be times when the fundamentals and real progress of high-quality BTCTCs temporarily disconnect from price. Over the long term, fundamentals and price tend to converge but especially during quieter summer periods, it’s important to stay conscious of these short-term inefficiencies

text: Research note from Tennyson Securities: Maintain BUY recommendation Capital Raise: SWC has raised just over £7.6 million via its ATM facility, with funds expected early this week. Use of Funds: Proceeds will be used to purchase additional Bitcoin, adding to its existing stack of 2,100 BTC. Placement Details: 3.45 million shares placed over the past two weeks at an average price of 221p per share. Bitcoin Price Impact: With Bitcoin near £90,000, SWC’s treasury has an unrealised gain of £18 million. Valuation: Shares trade slightly below 3x mNAV after recent sector-wide multiple compression. mNAV Outlook: Days to cover mNAV at a near-record low of 20 days due to strong BTC yield delivery. Price & NAV Growth: Expectation of strong growth in share price and mNAV in coming weeks. Recommendation: Maintain BUY.

text: Smarter Web Has 104 x’d Your Bitcoin Exposure in 15 weeks since IPO. Aug 8th: 1 SWC share = 724.88 satoshis July 16th: 1 SWC share = 616 satoshis July 11th: 1 SWC share = 526 satoshis July 7th: 1 SWC share = 412 satoshis May 7th: 1 SWC share = 7 satoshis To get 1 BTC exposure (100M sats): Aug 8th: 137,925 shares July 16th: 162,337 shares July 11th: 190,114 shares July 7th: 242,718 shares May 7th: 14,285,714 shares That’s a ~104x increase in sats per share in just 15 weeks - pure execution. This is why some investors are choosing $SWC over spot BTC or ETFs: as the company grows its BTC treasury without issuing more shares, you get more Bitcoin exposure per share. Calculation assumes 2,010 Bitcoin and 277,275,004 shares (fully diluted).

text: The progress that @asjwebley and @Croesus_BTC have made at Smarter Web, during what is typically one of the quietest periods of the year for the finance industry, is extremely impressive. I’ve mentioned this many times before (and will continue to) but it’s important to remember that markets are not perfectly efficient. Not every development is immediately priced in, especially during periods of low activity. But in time, fundamentals assert themselves. Whether it’s mid-September, when volumes historically begin to return, or the start of Q4 (often the strongest quarter for U.S. equities which lifts global sentiment), momentum finds its way back. What’s clear is that real progress is being made at $SWC . The conviction, energy, and long-term commitment of this community / investor base are tangible. With the foundations being laid today, we have every reason to stay confident in what’s ahead. Patience and persistence will make that even more evident.

text: Positive Research note from Tennyson Securities Main summary bullets below The Smarter Web Company (SWC PZ), a leading UK Bitcoin Treasury company, has raised US$21 million (£15.8 million) via a new Bitcoin-denominated convertible bond. The bond was fully subscribed by TOBAM, a Paris-based institutional investor with expertise in Bitcoin and Bitcoin Treasury Companies. Principal repayable after 12 months is indexed to Bitcoin’s price performance. TOBAM can convert its investment (partially or fully) into $SWC shares at 204.75p — a 5% premium to the last closing price — at any time during the term. SWC can call the bond after six months. The structure appeals to Bitcoin investors by protecting Bitcoin returns while offering upside to SWC share price performance. It benefits SWC by monetising share price volatility, raising capital at a premium, and expanding its Bitcoin treasury. SWC may issue more of these instruments, alongside ABBs, direct subscriptions, and its ATM facility. Bitcoin acquired via convertibles will be capped at 30% of existing unencumbered BTC holdings. The company is expected to surpass its 2,100 BTC target within days, making it the 25th largest public Bitcoin holder globally. Based on full deployment of cash into BTC and including dilution from the convert (7.72 million extra shares), shares now trade at an adjusted mNAV of 2.8x. SWC has delivered a BTC yield of 6.1% per day over the last 100 days. Days to mNAV cover is just 19 days — far quicker than peers like Metaplanet, The Blockchain Group, and MicroStrategy. The company’s rapid growth is attracting increasing global investor interest. While mNAV compression is expected over time, SWC’s strong daily yield supports a higher mNAV premium versus peers in the near term.
